verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a type I transmembrane protein of the p24 family that cycles between ER and Golgi as a cargo receptor in COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking — its dominant home is the early secretory pathway. However, NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization, and the protein has a documented surface pool reached via secretory cycling (it shuttles cargo to the PM and is detected at the cell surface). The TM topology places its GOLD-domain ectodomain on the extracellular face when at the PM. This is a minority PM pool alongside a dominant endomembrane compartment — classic dual_localization. Not yes (endomembrane-dominant, not a canonical surface receptor); not no (documented PM pool with own TM anchor).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether the PM pool is substantial/stable enough to support binder access, vs. transient transit en route to recycling back to Golgi.
